rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical selenylation/cyclization of ortho-hydroxyaryl alkenes with diselenides to give selenyl-dihydrobenzofurans (and a chromane) bearing a tetrasubstituted carbon center
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of the diaryl diselenide (PhSeSePh, 2a) to a radical cation that fragments into PhSe+ (cation B) and PhSe• (radical C); the PhSe+ engages the alkene as a seleniranium electrophile

key_intermediates.name: β-selenyl carbon radical E (from PhSe• addition to alkene)

electron_flow: The anode oxidizes PhSeSePh (lower oxidation potential than 1a per CV) to a radical cation that splits into PhSe+ (B) and PhSe• (C); B forms a seleniranium with the alkene (D), and intramolecular phenol attack with loss of H+ delivers the dihydrobenzofuran 3a. In parallel, PhSe• adds to the alkene to give radical E, which is anodically oxidized to D (or PhSe• is oxidized at the anode to regenerate B), while protons are reduced at the cathode to H2.

probe_reagents.name: PhSeCl (stoichiometric, no current)
probe_reagents.observation: Delivers 3a in 91% yield without electricity, supporting a seleniranium (PhSe+) intermediate

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: No effect on yield (3a still 84%), indicating free carbon radicals are not on the dominant pathway

notes: Authors favor Path 1 (anodic oxidation of diselenide to PhSe+, then seleniranium cyclization), consistent with PhSeCl control and TEMPO insensitivity. Two alternative pathways are proposed: Path 2 — PhSe• addition to alkene forming radical E, then anodic oxidation to seleniranium D; Path 3 — anodic oxidation of PhSe• to PhSe+ to re-enter the cationic cycle, with cathodic H2 evolution closing the redox balance.
